Subclasses can override this method to\\n1551:         customize the display of the choices.\\n1552:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1553:         return str(obj)\\n1554: \\n1555:     def _get_choices(self):\\n1556:         # If self._choices is set, then somebody must have manually set\\n1557:         # the property self.choices. In this case, just return self._choices.\\n1558:         if hasattr(self, \\\"_choices\\\"):\\n1559:             return self._choices\\n1560: \\n1561:         # Otherwise, execute the QuerySet in self.queryset to determine the\\n1562:         # choices dynamically. Return a fresh ModelChoiceIterator that has not\\n1563:         # been consumed. Note that we're instantiating a new\\n1564:         # ModelChoiceIterator *each* time _get_choices() is called (and, thus,\\n1565:         # each time self.choices is accessed) so that we can ensure the\\n1566:         # QuerySet has not been consumed. This construct might look complicated\\n1567:         # but it allows for lazy evaluation of the queryset.\\n1568:         return self.iterator(self)\\n1569: \\n1570:     choices = {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01FdCebi7szC8QPVexBGRrxM","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Found it! The issue is at line 120 in model_to_dict:```python\ndata[f.name] = f.value_from_object(instance)\n```\n\nFor ForeignKey fields, `value_from_object` gets the actual field value (which is the primary key by default). However, when the ForeignKey is using `to_field`, we need to get the value from the to_field instead.\n\nNow let me think about this more carefully. When a ModelForm is created with an instance:1. model_to_dict gets called and gets the ForeignKey value - this is the PK of the related object2. This value becomes the initial value for the form field3. When the BoundField renders, it calls field.prepare_value() on this initial value4. prepare_value sees that it's not a model instance (it's a PK value), so it just returns it as-is\n\nBut the choices for the ModelChoiceField use barcode values like 86, 22, 87. So the selected value (1, 2, 3 - the PKs) won't match any of the choice values (86, 22, 87).\n\nThe fix is:\nOption 1: Modify model_to_dict to use the to_field value instead of the pk when dealing with ForeignKey fields.\nOption 2: Modify the ModelChoiceField to convert the initial value properly.\n\nLet me think about which is better. Looking at the code, the prepare_value method in ModelChoiceField already handles the case where the value is a model instance. But when the value is a PK (from model_to_dict), it doesn't know how to convert it.\n\nActually, I think the right place to fix this is in the ModelChoiceField.prepare_value method. When it receives a value that's not a model instance but is just the PK, it should convert it to the to_field value.\n\nBut wait, prepare_value can only do this if it has access to the model instance. \n\nThe real problem is in the data flow: model_to_dict returns the PK (1) instead of the to_field value (86). When prepare_value receives just the PK without the model instance, it can't look up what the corresponding to_field value should be. The solution needs to be in model_to_dict itself—it should fetch the to_field value directly when a ForeignKey uses a custom to_field, rather than relying on the PK.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":8,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1599,"cache_read_input_tokens":93969,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1599},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ad7551e-6f93-49d5-9f85-4af297977137","uuid":"cef18b39-111e-4512-a957-f3265cc65352"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_012nYh4B9jwQEzEkHP9HCWcD","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Perfect! I found it! In django/db/models/fields/__init__.py, at lines 1149-1151:\n\n```python\ndef value_from_object(self, obj):\n    \"\"\"Return the value of this field in the given model instance.\"\"\"\n    return getattr(obj, self.attname)\n```\n\nAnd in django/db/models/fields/related.py, at lines 1254-1255:\n\n```python\ndef get_attname(self):\n    return \"%s_id\" % self.name\n```\n\nSo for a ForeignKey field, `attname` is the field name with \"_id\" appended (e.g., \"parent_id\"). This is the column in the database that stores the foreign key value (the PK of the related object).\n\nThe issue is that `model_to_dict` calls `f.value_from_object(instance)` which returns the PK value, but when the ForeignKey uses `to_field`, we need to return the value of the `to_field` instead.\n\nThe fix should be in the `model_to_dict` function. When handling ForeignKey fields, we need to check if the field has a non-default `to_field` and if so, get the value from the related object's to_field instead of using the attname.\n\nHowever, looking at this more carefully, I realize there's another approach. Instead of modifying model_to_dict, we could modify the ModelChoiceField.prepare_value method to handle the case where the field has a to_field and the value passed is a PK (not a model instance).\n\nBut actually, the prepare_value method can't know what the PK is without accessing the database or the instance. So it's better to fix it in model_to_dict.\n\nLooking at the code again, when model_to_dict is called, it has access to the instance, so it can get the related object and extract the to_field value.\n\nActually, wait. Let me think about this more carefully. \n\nThe ForeignKey stores the PK of the related object in the database via attname. When model_to_dict retrieves the value using `f.value_from_object(instance)`, it gets that PK. For fields with a custom to_field, I need to fetch the related object and extract the to_field value instead. Looking at the test case at line 3057 where `model_to_dict(core)` is called directly, the fix definitely belongs in model_to_dict itself.\n\nI should examine how form fields are generated for ForeignKey fields in fields_for_model to understand the full picture.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":8,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2241,"cache_read_input_tokens":101129,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2241},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ad7551e-6f93-49d5-9f85-4af297977137","uuid":"519bc2ea-a38f-4f53-9c8c-05d14507549f"}

Let me look at the actual failing test to understand what data flow is happening. When model_to_dict is called for the `core` instance which has `parent=apple`, what should it return?\n\nLooking at the test:\n```python\ncore = Inventory.objects.create(barcode=87, name=\"Core\", parent=apple)\n```\n\nWhere apple has barcode=86. So core.parent_id would be the PK of apple (let's say 1).\n\nWhen we call model_to_dict(core), it currently returns {parent: 1}.\n\nBut the form field expects the value to be 86 (the barcode), since the ForeignKey uses to_field=\"barcode\".\n\nSo the fix is: in model_to_dict, when we encounter a ForeignKey field, we need to check if it has a custom to_field, and if so, get the related object and return the value of the to_field instead of the PK.\n\nHere's the implementation:\n1. Check if the field is a ForeignKey with a remote_field2. Get the related object: getattr(instance, f.name)\n3. If the related object is not None, get the to_field value: getattr(related_object, to_field_name)\n4. You can\\n604:     optionally pass a `form` argument to use as a starting point for\\n605:     constructing the ModelForm.\\n606: \\n607:     ``fields`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, include only\\n608:     the named fields in the returned fields. If omitted or '__all__', use all\\n609:     fields.\\n610: \\n611:     ``exclude`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, exclude the\\n612:     named fields from the returned fields, even if they are listed in the\\n613:     ``fields`` argument.\\n614: \\n615:     ``widgets`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a widget.\\n616: \\n617:     ``localized_fields`` is a list of names of fields which should be\\n618:     localized.\\n619: \\n620:     ``formfield_callback`` is a callable that takes a model field and returns\\n621:     a form field.\\n622: \\n623:     ``labels`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a label.\\n624: \\n625:     ``help_texts`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a help text.\\n626: \\n627:     ``error_messages`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a\\n628:     dictionary of error messages.\\n629: \\n630:     ``field_classes`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a form\\n631:     field class.\\n632:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n633:     # Create the inner Meta class. Fields #####################################################################\\n1373: \\n1374: \\n1375: class InlineForeignKeyField(Field):\\n1376:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1377:     A basic integer field that deals with validating the given value to a\\n1378:     given parent instance in an inline.\\n1379:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1380: \\n1381:     widget = HiddenInput\\n1382:     default_error_messages = {\\n1383:         \\\"invalid_choice\\\": _(\\\"The inline value did not match the parent instance.\\\"),\\n1384:     }\\n1385: \\n1386:     def __init__(self, parent_instance, *args, pk_field=False, to_field=None, **kwargs):\\n1387:         self.parent_instance = parent_instance\\n1388:         self.pk_field = pk_field\\n1389:         self.to_field = to_field\\n1390:         if self.parent_instance is not None:\\n1391:             if self.to_field:\\n1392:                 kwargs[\\\"initial\\\"] = getattr(self.parent_instance, self.to_field)\\n1393:             else:\\n1394:                 kwargs[\\\"initial\\\"] = self.parent_instance.pk\\n1395:         kwargs[\\\"required\\\"] = False\\n1396:         super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)\\n1397: \\n1398:     def clean(self, value):\\n1399:         if value in self.empty_values:\\n1400:             if self.pk_field:\\n1401:                 return None\\n1402:             # if there is no value act as we did before.\\n1403:             return self.parent_instance\\n1404:         # ensure the we compare the values as equal types.\\n1405:         if self.to_field:\\n1406:             orig = getattr(self.parent_instance, self.to_field)\\n1407:         else:\\n1408:             orig = self.parent_instance.pk\\n1409:         if str(value) != str(orig):\\n1410:             raise ValidationError(\\n1411:                 self.error_messages[\\\"invalid_choice\\\"], code=\\\"invalid_choice\\\"\\n1412:             )\\n1413:         return self.parent_instance\\n1414: \\n1415:     def has_changed(self, initial, data):\\n1416:         return False\\n1417: \\n1418: \\n1419: class ModelChoiceIteratorValue:\\n1420:     def __init__(self, value, instance):\\n1421:         self.value = value\\n1422:         self.instance = instance\\n1423: \\n1424:     def __str__(self):\\n1425:         return str(self.value)\\n1426: \\n1427:     def __hash__(self):\\n1428:         return hash(self.value)\\n1429: \\n1430:     def __eq__(self, other):\\n1431:         if isinstance(other, ModelChoiceIteratorValue):\\n1432:             other = other.value\\n1433:         return self.value == other\\n1434: \\n1435: \\n1436: class ModelChoiceIterator(BaseChoiceIterator):\\n1437:     def __init__(self, field):\\n1438:         self.field = field\\n1439:         self.queryset = field.queryset\\n1440: \\n1441:     def __iter__(self):\\n1442:         if self.field.empty_label is not None:\\n1443:             yield (\\\"\\\", self.field.empty_label)\\n1444:         queryset = self.queryset\\n1445:         # Can't use iterator() when queryset uses prefetch_related()\\n1446:         if not queryset._prefetch_related_lookups:\\n1447:             queryset = queryset.iterator()\\n1448:         for obj in queryset:\\n1449:             yield self.choice(obj)\\n1450: \\n1451:     def __len__(self):\\n1452:         # Now I need to write the fix. The issue is in the `model_to_dict` function (lines 99-121). \n\nThe problem is:\n1. When a ForeignKey uses `to_field` parameter to reference a non-PK field\n2. `model_to_dict` calls `f.value_from_object(instance)` which returns the PK value (from the `attname` which is the database column, not the related object's field)\n3. But the form field's choices use the `to_field` value (e.g., \"code\" field)\n4. So when the widget compares values, it gets: form value=\"US\" (the code), initial value=1 (the PK), they don't match, so nothing is selected\n\nThe fix should be:\n- When we encounter a ForeignKey field, get the actual related object\n- From that object, get the value of the `to_field_name` (which is `f.remote_field.field_name`)\n- Use that value as the initial value instead of the PK\n\nLet me now write the actual file with this fix.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":9,"cache_creation_input_tokens":77876,"cache_read_input_tokens":30587,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":77876},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ad7551e-6f93-49d5-9f85-4af297977137","uuid":"441033b3-760e-48a0-b254-a6ee688a84d0"}

    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n55:     from django.db import models\\n56: \\n57:     opts = instance._meta\\n58: \\n59:     cleaned_data = form.cleaned_data\\n60:     file_field_list = []\\n61:     for f in \\n96: # ModelForms #################################################################\\n97: \\n98: \\n99: def model_to_dict(instance, fields=None, exclude=None):\\n100:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n101: See tickets #12507, #12521, #12553.\\n400:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n401:         exclude = set()\\n402:         # Build up a list of fields that should be excluded from model field\\n403:         # validation and unique checks.\\n404:         for f in self.instance._meta.fields:\\n405:             field = f.name\\n406:             # Exclude fields that aren't on the form. The developer may be\\n407:             # adding these values to the model after form validation.\\n408:             if field not in self.fields:\\n409:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n410: \\n411:             # Don't perform model validation on fields that were defined\\n412:             # manually on the form and excluded via the ModelForm's Meta\\n413:             # class. See #12901.\\n414:             elif self._meta.fields and field not in self._meta.fields:\\n415:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n416:             elif self._meta.exclude and field in self._meta.exclude:\\n417:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n418: \\n419:             # Exclude fields that failed form validation. There's no need for\\n420:             # the model fields to validate them as well.\\n421:             elif field in self._errors:\\n422:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n423: \\n424:             # Exclude empty fields that are not required by the form, if the\\n425:             # underlying model field is required. This keeps the model field\\n426:             # from raising a required error. Note: don't exclude the field from\\n427:             # validation if the model field allows blanks. If it does, the\\n428:             # blank value may be included in a unique check, so cannot be\\n429:             # excluded from validation.\\n430:             else:\\n431:                 form_field = self.fields[field]\\n432:                 field_value = self.cleaned_data.get(field)\\n433:                 if (\\n434:                     not f.blank\\n435:                     and not form_field.required\\n436:                     and field_value in form_field.empty_values\\n437:                 ):\\n438:                     exclude.add(f.name)\\n439:         return exclude\\n440: \\n441:     def clean(self):\\n442:         self._validate_unique = True\\n443:         self._validate_constraints = True\\n444:         return self.cleaned_data\\n445: \\n446:     def _update_errors(self, errors):\\n447:         # Override any validation error messages defined at the model level\\n448:         # with those defined at the form level.\\n449:         opts = self._meta\\n450: \\n451:         # Allow the model generated by construct_instance() to raise\\n452:         # ValidationError and have them handled in the same way as others.\\n453:         if hasattr(errors, \\\"error_dict\\\"):\\n454:             error_dict = errors.error_dict\\n455:         else:\\n456:             error_dict = {NON_FIELD_ERRORS: errors}\\n457: \\n458:         for field, messages in error_dict.items():\\n459:             if (\\n460:                 field == NON_FIELD_ERRORS\\n461:                 and opts.error_messages\\n462:                 and NON_FIELD_ERRORS in opts.error_messages\\n463:             ):\\n464:                 error_messages = opts.error_messages[NON_FIELD_ERRORS]\\n465:             elif field in self.fields:\\n466:                 error_messages = self.fields[field].error_messages\\n467:             else:\\n468:                 continue\\n469: \\n470:             for message in messages:\\n471:                 if (\\n472:                     isinstance(message, ValidationError)\\n473:                     and message.code in error_messages\\n474:                 ):\\n475:                     message.message = error_messages[message.code]\\n476: \\n477:         self.add_error(None, errors)\\n478: \\n479:     def _post_clean(self):\\n480:         opts = self._meta\\n481: \\n482:         exclude = self._get_validation_exclusions()\\n483: \\n484:         # Foreign Keys being used to represent inline relationships\\n485:         # are excluded from basic field value validation. This is for two\\n486:         # reasons: firstly, the value may not be supplied (#12507; the\\n487:         # case of providing new values to the admin); secondly the\\n488:         # object being referred to may not yet fully exist (#12749).\\n489:         # However, these fields *must* be included in uniqueness checks,\\n490:         # so this can't be part of _get_validation_exclusions().\\n491:         for name, field in self.fields.items():\\n492:             if isinstance(field, InlineForeignKeyField):\\n493:                 exclude.add(name)\\n494: \\n495:         try:\\n496:             self.instance = construct_instance(\\n497:                 self, self.instance, opts.fields, opts.exclude\\n498:             )\\n499:         except ValidationError as e:\\n500:             self._update_errors(e)\\n501: \\n502:         try:\\n503:             self.instance.full_clean(\\n504:                 exclude=exclude, validate_unique=False, validate_constraints=False\\n505:             )\\n506:         except ValidationError as e:\\n507:             self._update_errors(e)\\n508: \\n509:         # Validate uniqueness and constraints if needed.\\n510:         if self._validate_unique:\\n511:             self.validate_unique()\\n512:         if self._validate_constraints:\\n513:             self.validate_constraints()\\n514: \\n515:     def validate_unique(self):\\n516:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n517:         Call the instance's validate_unique() method and update the form's\\n518:         validation errors if any were raised.\\n519:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n520:         exclude = self._get_validation_exclusions()\\n521:         try:\\n522:             self.instance.validate_unique(exclude=exclude)\\n523:         except ValidationError as e:\\n524:             self._update_errors(e)\\n525: \\n526:     def validate_constraints(self):\\n527:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n528:         Call the instance's validate_constraints() method and update the form's\\n529:         validation errors if any were raised.\\n530:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n531:         exclude = self._get_validation_exclusions()\\n532:         try:\\n533:             self.instance.validate_constraints(exclude=exclude)\\n534:         except ValidationError as e:\\n535:             self._update_errors(e)\\n536: \\n537:     def _save_m2m(self):\\n538:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n539:         Save the many-to-many fields and generic relations for this form.\\n540:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n541:         cleaned_data = self.cleaned_data\\n542:         exclude = self._meta.exclude\\n543:         fields = self._meta.fields\\n544:         opts = self.instance._meta\\n545:         # Note that for historical reasons we want to include also\\n546:         # private_fields here. (GenericRelation was previously a fake\\n547:         # m2m field).\\n548:         for f in chain(opts.many_to_many, opts.private_fields):\\n549:             if not hasattr(f, \\\"save_form_data\\\"):\\n550:                 continue\\n551:             if fields and f.name not in fields:\\n552:                 continue\\n553:             if exclude and f.name in exclude:\\n554:                 continue\\n555:             if f.name in cleaned_data:\\n556:                 f.save_form_data(self.instance, cleaned_data[f.name])\\n557: \\n558:     def save(self, commit=True):\\n559:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n560:         Save this form's self.instance object if commit=True. Otherwise, add\\n561:         a save_m2m() method to the form which can be called after the instance\\n562:         is saved manually at a later time. Return the model instance.\\n563:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n564:         if self.errors:\\n565:             raise ValueError(\\n566:                 \\\"The %s could not be %s because the data didn't validate.\\\"\\n567:                 % (\\n568:                     self.instance._meta.object_name,\\n569:                     \\\"created\\\" if self.instance._state.adding else \\\"changed\\\",\\n570:                 )\\n571:             )\\n572:         if commit:\\n573:             # If committing, save the instance and the m2m data immediately.\\n574:             self.instance.save()\\n575:             self._save_m2m()\\n576:         else:\\n577:             # If not committing, add a method to the form to allow deferred\\n578:             # saving of m2m data.\\n579:             self.save_m2m = self._save_m2m\\n580:         return self.instance\\n581: \\n582:     save.alters_data = True\\n583: \\n584: \\n585: class ModelForm(BaseModelForm, metaclass=ModelFormMetaclass):\\n586:     pass\\n587: \\n588: \\n589: def modelform_factory(\\n590:     model,\\n591:     form=ModelForm,\\n592:     fields=None,\\n593:     exclude=None,\\n594:     formfield_callback=None,\\n595:     widgets=None,\\n596:     localized_fields=None,\\n597:     labels=None,\\n598:     help_texts=None,\\n599:     error_messages=None,\\n600:     field_classes=None,\\n601: ):\\n602:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n603:     Return a ModelForm containing form fields for the given model. You can\\n604:     optionally pass a `form` argument to use as a starting point for\\n605:     constructing the ModelForm.\\n606: \\n607:     ``fields`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, include only\\n608:     the named fields in the returned fields. If omitted or '__all__', use all\\n609:     fields.\\n610: \\n611:     ``exclude`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, exclude the\\n612:     named fields from the returned fields, even if they are listed in the\\n613:     ``fields`` argument.\\n614: \\n615:     ``widgets`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a widget.\\n616: \\n617:     ``localized_fields`` is a list of names of fields which should be\\n618:     localized.\\n619: \\n620:     ``formfield_callback`` is a callable that takes a model field and returns\\n621:     a form field.\\n622: \\n623:     ``labels`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a label.\\n624: \\n625:     ``help_texts`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a help text.\\n626: \\n627:     ``error_messages`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a\\n628:     dictionary of error messages.\\n629: \\n630:     ``field_classes`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a form\\n631:     field class.\\n632:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n633:     # Create the inner Meta class. FIXME: ideally, we should be able to\\n634:     # construct a ModelForm without creating and passing in a temporary\\n635:     # inner class.\\n636: \\n637:     # Build up a list of attributes that the Meta object will have.\\n638:     attrs = {\\\"model\\\": model}\\n639:     if fields is not None:\\n640:         attrs[\\\"fields\\\"] = fields\\n641:     if exclude is not None:\\n642:         attrs[\\\"exclude\\\"] = exclude\\n643:     if widgets is not None:\\n644:         attrs[\\\"widgets\\\"] = widgets\\n645:     if localized_fields is not None:\\n646:            try:\\n741:                     pk = self.data[pk_key]\\n742:                 except KeyError:\\n743:                     # The primary key is missing. Collect unique_checks and date_checks to run from all the forms.\\n826:         all_unique_checks = set()\\n827:         all_date_checks = set()\\n828:         forms_to_delete = self.deleted_forms\\n829:         valid_forms = [\\n830:             form\\n831:             for form in self.forms\\n832:             if form.is_valid() and form not in forms_to_delete\\n833:         ]\\n834:         for form in valid_forms:\\n835:             exclude = form._get_validation_exclusions()\\n836:             unique_checks, date_checks = form.instance._get_unique_checks(\\n837:                 exclude=exclude,\\n838:                 include_meta_constraints=True,\\n839:             )\\n840:             all_unique_checks.update(unique_checks)\\n841:             all_date_checks.update(date_checks)\\n842: \\n843:         errors = []\\n844:         # the form as invalid\\n871:                         errors.append(self.get_unique_error_message(unique_check))\\n872:                         form._errors[NON_FIELD_ERRORS] = self.error_class(\\n873:                             [self.get_form_error()],\\n874:                             renderer=self.renderer,\\n875:                         )\\n876:                         # Remove the data from the cleaned_data dict since it\\n877:                         # was invalid.\\n878:                         for field in unique_check:\\n879:                             if field in form.cleaned_data:\\n880:                                 del form.cleaned_data[field]\\n881:                     # The object was already deleted from the database.\\n962:             if not obj._is_pk_set():\\n963:                 continue\\n964:             if form in forms_to_delete:\\n965:                 self.deleted_objects.append(obj)\\n966:                 self.delete_existing(obj, commit=commit)\\n967:             elif form.has_changed():\\n968:                 self.changed_objects.append((obj, form.changed_data))\\n969:                 saved_instances.append(self.save_existing(form, obj, commit=commit))\\n970:                 if not commit:\\n971:                     self.saved_forms.append(form)\\n972:         return saved_instances\\n973: \\n974:     def save_new_objects(self, commit=True):\\n975:         self.new_objects = []\\n976:         for form in self.extra_forms:\\n977:             if not form.has_changed():\\n978:                 continue\\n979:             # If someone has marked an add form for deletion, don't save the\\n980:             # object.\\n981:             if self.can_delete and self._should_delete_form(form):\\n982:                 continue\\n983:             self.new_objects.append(self.save_new(form, commit=commit))\\n984:             if not commit:\\n985:                 self.saved_forms.append(form)\\n986:         return self.new_objects\\n987: \\n988:     def add_fields(self, form, index):\\n989:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Add a hidden field for the object's primary key.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n990:         from django.db.models import AutoField, ForeignKey, OneToOneField\\n991: \\n992:         self._pk_field = pk = self.model._meta.pk\\n993:         # If a pk isn't editable, then it won't be on the form, so we need to\\n994:         # add it here so we can tell which object is which when we get the\\n995:         # data back.            }\\n1197: \\n1198:         # The InlineForeignKeyField assumes that the foreign key relation is\\n1199:         # based on the parent model's pk. If fk_name is\\n1231:     provided, assume it is the name of the ForeignKey field. Unless can_fail is\\n1232:     True, raise an exception if there isn't a ForeignKey from model to\\n1233:     parent_model.\\n1234:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1235:     # avoid circular import\\n1236:     from django.db.models import ForeignKey\\n1237: \\n1238:     opts = model._meta\\n1239:     if fk_name:\\n1240:         fks_to_parent = [f for f in opts.fields if f.name == fk_name]\\n1241:         if len(fks_to_parent) == 1:\\n1242:             fk = fks_to_parent[0]\\n1243:             all_parents = (*parent_model._meta.all_parents, parent_model)\\n1244:             if (\\n1245:                 not isinstance(fk, ForeignKey)\\n1246:                 or (\\n1247:                     #
